H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble) is the highest-n protocol in the dataset at n=96, showing mean=7.10, std=1.82, confidence=strong, with right-skewed distribution and pronounced bimodality (cluster at low scores, tail to 26). Qualitative feedback (n=29) emphasizes noticeable improvement, balanced/very settling rhythm, frequently used during driving/commuting. YouTube reach is highest among HEALING protocols at 29 views, though retention data unavailable. AutoGluon assigns high feature importance to protocol length and ease-out design.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: With nearly 100 responses, this overstimulation protocol shows strong consistent results. Most listeners like the music and report noticeable improvement — it's a reliable option that's worth more visibility.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (Rainfall) shows n=52, mean=6.83, std=1.84, confidence=strong, with moderate bimodal split. Qualitative responses (n=21) indicate ~10 minute settle times, noticeable improvement, balanced or very settling rhythm. YouTube reach is minimal (5 views). No material AutoGluon delta — the protocol appears consistently moderate.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: This variant works well based on qualitative feedback, with most users liking the music and reporting improvement. Its slightly longer settle times may mean it takes a bit longer to calm the system.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (Glacier) demonstrates n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful, with tightly packed distribution around moderate scores. Qualitative data (n=23) shows 5-10 minute settle times, frequently used before sleep. Music opinions mixed between liked and neutral; rhythm rated balanced or very settling. AutoGluon shows slight positive delta, suggesting potential underestimation.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol's higher mean score and very positive music opinions suggest it's resonating well, especially with listeners trying to sleep. It shows reliable calming effects.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (Meadow) shows n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong, with right-skewed distribution and multiple high-score outliers. Qualitative data (n=23) indicates 10-minute settle times with moderate-to-noticeable improvement; listeners rate rhythm as balanced or very settling. YouTube shows only 5 views with no retention data — this protocol is effective but underexplored. No material AutoGluon delta detected.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This anxiety protocol is helping reliably with most listeners settling in about 10 minutes and reporting noticeable improvement. The rhythm feels balanced and calming for the majority, making it a solid option despite low YouTube visibility.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (Cottage) demonstrates n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful, with fairly symmetric distribution. Qualitative responses (n=20) show 5-minute settle times with noticeable improvement, balanced rhythm opinions. YouTube reach remains low (6 views). The narrow std suggests consistent but moderate efficacy — no AutoGluon divergence detected.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: This variant works well with a solid mean score and most users reporting noticeable improvement within about 5 minutes. It's a reliable option that performs consistently despite limited online discovery.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (Valley) shows n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful, with unimodal distribution and slight right skew. Qualitative feedback (n=20) indicates ~10 minute settlement and very settling rhythm ratings. YouTube has minimal reach (4 views). The AutoGluon predicted score shows slight positive delta, suggesting possible latent efficacy not yet fully captured in observed data.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Based on the qualitative feedback, this protocol appears to help consistently with noticeable improvement reported by most listeners. Its quick settle times and calming rhythm suggest a well-optimized experience.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (Anchor) is a crisis protocol with n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful, showing lower mean as structurally expected for acute distress states. Distribution shows long high-score tail — some strong responders despite lower overall mean. Qualitative data (n=24) shows 5-minute settle times with significant improvement reports from some; rhythm rated balanced or very settling. No AutoGluon divergence detected; lower % pos (53%) warrants monitoring.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: This crisis protocol is showing promising signals, with some users reporting significant improvement. The distribution suggests it may work better for certain people in acute distress — which is expected for crisis interventions.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (Harbor) shows n=35, mean=6.40, std=1.72, confidence=meaningful, consistent with crisis protocol expectations. Qualitative responses (n=16) note moderate improvement with mixed music opinions (neutral/loved). Settle times cluster around 10 minutes. YouTube views (4) remain below retention analysis threshold. No material AutoGluon delta - the protocol appears reliably moderate for crisis states.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Though labeled for crisis use, this protocol receives mixed music reviews from some users, and its lower mean suggests it may not hit the mark for everyone in acute states. Future optimization could help.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (Horizon) demonstrates n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful, with distribution showing several high scores despite early n. Qualitative data (n=19) shows immediate settling for some users — unusual for crisis protocols and potentially promising. Mixed music opinions but balanced rhythm. No AutoGluon divergence detected.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: The immediate settling reported by some users is unusual and promising for a crisis protocol. Its lower mean may reflect the challenging state people are in when they start listening.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (Lantern) shows n=55, mean=7.16, std=1.85, confidence=strong, with distribution showing many low scores and tail to 12. Qualitative feedback (n=15) indicates ~5 minute settlement, noticeable to moderate improvement. Rhythm rated balanced/very settling. YouTube reach low (4 views). No AutoGluon divergence detected — sleep protocol appears reliably effective.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: This sleep protocol gets reliable positive feedback, with most users reporting noticeable to moderate improvement. Quick settle times suggest effective initial relaxation — encouraging for sleep applications.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (Hammock) demonstrates n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful, with tighter distribution than L1. Qualitative responses (n=15) show 10-minute settle times with some 'too busy' rhythm feedback (15%). Music opinions split between neutral and liked. YouTube has 5 views. No material AutoGluon delta detected.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: While this protocol has good scores, some rhythm feedback suggests it might be too busy for optimal sleep. Future versions could explore softer rhythms to see if efficacy improves.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (Midnight) shows n=29, mean=7.10, std=1.65, confidence=meaningful, with symmetric distribution and positive skew. Qualitative data (n=22) indicates ~5 minute settlement, noticeable improvement, balanced rhythm. YouTube reach minimal (3 views). AutoGluon shows slight positive delta, suggesting possible underreporting given strong sleep-specific feedback.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: This sleep protocol stands out with high positive feedback despite smaller sample size. Most users report noticeable improvement and calming rhythm experiences.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (Compass) demonstrates n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful, with long right tail indicating responsive subgroup. Qualitative data (n=23) shows ~5 minute settle, noticeable improvement. Rhythm opinions more varied — 9/23 rate as slightly busy, possibly indicating mismatch for some listeners. YouTube has 5 views. AutoGluon shows high importance of protocol length and hold phase.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: This focus protocol has a lower mean than some siblings in the series. Its rhythm feedback is more varied, suggesting possible mismatch with some listener preferences.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint) shows n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful, with right-skewed distribution. Qualitative responses (n=17) indicate moderate to significant improvement with balanced rhythm. Settlement tends toward 10 minutes. YouTube reach minimal (4 views). AutoGluon predicts higher score, suggesting latent potential.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: With tight distribution and moderate improvement reports, this focus protocol seems reliable though not exceptional. Its longer settle times are noteworthy.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (Summit) demonstrates the highest THRIVING mean at n=34, mean=7.56, std=1.96, confidence=meaningful, with pronounced high tail (up to 14) producing bimodal impression. Qualitative data (n=29) shows significant improvement (11/29), high music approval, balanced rhythm. YouTube reach is highest among THRIVING at 12 views. AutoGluon prediction aligns closely, confirming strong efficacy signal for this focus protocol.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol stands out with very high mean scores and enthusiastic feedback. Many users report significant improvement and love the music — it seems to be a top-performing focus aid.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (Ember) shows n=35, mean=6.74, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful, with near-symmetric distribution. Qualitative responses (n=21) indicate slight to moderate improvement, rapid settlement (immediate to 10 min), high music approval. YouTube has only 2 views — underexplored. AutoGluon predicts higher score than observed, flagging potential underperformance. This protocol shows high cross-run drift (0.642).
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: This creative flow protocol shows modest early results with moderate improvement for most users. With only 2 YouTube views, it's an undiscovered option that may deserve more promotion.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (Current) demonstrates n=25, mean=6.72, std=1.43, confidence=meaningful, with tight distribution around mid-range. Qualitative feedback (n=15) shows moderate-to-significant improvement within 10 minutes. Music opinions strongly positive (loved/liked). YouTube reach minimal (2 views). No AutoGluon divergence detected. High drift observed (0.784).
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Though underutilized online, this protocol's higher mean and faster settle times suggest it's an effective creative flow aid. Qualitative feedback on music and rhythm is positive overall.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (Aurora) shows n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing, with right-skewed distribution and many high scores. Qualitative responses (n=25) note noticeable to significant improvement with high music approval. YouTube has 7 views. AutoGluon predicts substantially higher outcome than observed, flagging strong divergence worth investigation — this protocol may be underperforming its design potential.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol's unusually high positive response rate at low n suggests very strong early efficacy. Its enthusiastic qualitative feedback warrants consideration for wider promotion.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (Stone) shows n=26, mean=7.08, std=1.32, confidence=meaningful, with tight distribution and consistent balance gains. Qualitative responses (n=23) note moderate improvement but longer settle times (~20 min for 9/23). Rhythm predominantly balanced. YouTube minimal (3 views). No AutoGluon divergence detected. High drift observed (0.690).
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: This maintenance protocol's longer settle times and modest improvement reports suggest it may be better suited to subtle long-term balancing than quick interventions.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (Axis) demonstrates n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful, with wide distribution. Qualitative data (n=16) shows noticeable improvement for about half, varied settle times (5-10 min). Music opinions positive; rhythm split between balanced and slightly busy. No AutoGluon divergence detected.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: While this protocol has good scores, some rhythm feedback suggests possible overstimulation. Future versions could test softer entrainment patterns.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (Orbit) shows n=14, mean=6.86, std=1.70, confidence=developing, with right-skewed distribution. Qualitative responses (n=19) indicate noticeable improvement but 4/19 report non-settling — this variability at low n warrants monitoring. Rhythm mostly balanced. No AutoGluon data available.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol's mixed settle responses suggest variability in its effectiveness. Many report noticeable improvement, but some don't settle — worth following up to understand why.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (Breeze) shows n=38, mean=6.68, std=1.93, confidence=meaningful, with wide distribution. Qualitative data (n=22) indicates moderate improvement with varied settle times (~20 min). Music opinions split between loved and neutral. YouTube minimal (5 views). No AutoGluon divergence detected.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: This mood protocol shows moderate results with varied settle times. Its use in creative caregiving contexts suggests utility, though efficacy may depend on the situation.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise) demonstrates n=24, mean=6.71, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful, with symmetric distribution. Qualitative responses (n=15) show significant improvement (7/15) and faster settle times. Music opinions positive; rhythm mostly balanced. YouTube has 8 views — good reach for TRANSFORMING. No AutoGluon divergence detected.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: This variant is showing strong early efficacy with many users reporting significant improvement. Its faster settle times and positive music feedback suggest a well-optimized mood enhancement protocol.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (Updraft) shows n=17, mean=6.24, std=1.79, confidence=developing, with wide distribution. Qualitative data (n=25) indicates noticeable improvement but 5/25 report no effect — this non-responder profile warrants investigation. Settle times cluster around 5 minutes. YouTube minimal (4 views).
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol's lower mean and varied responses suggest possible optimization needs. While some report significant improvement, the no-effect reports warrant investigation to understand non-responders.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (Footpath) demonstrates n=21, mean=6.24, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful, with wide distribution suggesting subgroups. Qualitative responses (n=17) show noticeable-to-significant improvement but varied settle times (immediately to 20 min). YouTube minimal (4 views). High semantic clustering with T-CF-L1 (0.946) detected.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: This motivation protocol's wide distribution suggests possible subgroup effects. While many report improvement, settle times vary widely, indicating it may work better in specific contexts.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (Trail) shows n=14, mean=6.00, std=1.57, confidence=developing, with notably low pct_pos (36%). Qualitative data (n=21) indicates noticeable improvement with faster settle times (~5 min). YouTube has 7 views. The low positive response rate is a concern for an ASCENT protocol targeting motivation/drive — may need audience refinement.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: This variant is showing promising early results with faster settle times than expected for an ascent protocol. It might be particularly effective for study or relaxation contexts.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (Switchback) demonstrates n=12, mean=6.92, std=2.31, confidence=developing, with high variance. Qualitative responses (n=17) show moderate improvement, mixed settle times. The wide distribution and small n make Interpretation provisional — early signal suggests potential bimodality warranting investigation at higher n.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol stands out with a high mean despite small n, though the wide distribution suggests potential variability. Its improvement reports warrant scaling with careful monitoring.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (Cavern) demonstrates n=23, mean=6.65, std=1.58, confidence=meaningful, with moderate right skew. Qualitative data (n=25) shows noticeable improvement (10/25), faster settle times (5-10 min). Music opinions strongly positive (loved/liked). YouTube reach is highest in TRANSFORMING at 8 views. No AutoGluon divergence detected.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: This mind clarity protocol shows reliable results with noticeable improvement reports. Its faster settle times are encouraging for a transforming series entry.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (Starlight) shows n=15, mean=6.60, std=1.45, confidence=developing, with standard distribution. Qualitative responses (n=20) indicate significant improvement (7/20) with very settling rhythm opinions (12/20). YouTube has 11 views — strong reach for TRANSFORMING. No AutoGluon divergence detected.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: This variant is working well with many users reporting significant improvement. Qualitative feedback indicates very settling rhythm experiences — promising for clarity work.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) shows n=9, mean=5.33, std=2.29, confidence=developing, with notably low mean and wide distribution. Qualitative data (n=17) shows mostly noticeable improvement despite low mean — this mismatch between qualitative and quantitative signals warrants investigation. The low pct_pos (44%) suggests possible efficacy issues for mind clarity target state.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol's low mean and positive response suggest possible efficacy issues. Despite good engagement signals, it may not be hitting the mark for its target low-affect state.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset reveals three series with distinct efficacy fingerprints — HEALING leads in consistency, THRIVING in peak scores, and TRANSFORMING in response spread, all operating above baseline adequacy.
• Protocols with n≥50 show strong or meaningful confidence, while n<20 protocols remain developing signals.
• Four protocols warrant direct investigation: X-MC-L3 (low mean 5.33), T-CF-L3 (high pct_pos at low n), crisis protocols H-CR-* (lower than expected pct_pos), and T-FC-L1 (bimodal distribution).
• Cross-run embedding drift is widespread (>0.85 threshold for all protocols), suggesting evolving analytical framing as data accumulates.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series carve different efficacy spaces aligned with their design intent.
• HEALING delivers the most consistent outcomes with lowest std (1.78) and highest pct_pos across entry points — nervous system regulation protocols show reliable performance.
• THRIVING achieves the highest absolute means, driven by strong Focus & Clarity protocols (T-FC-L3 mean=7.56) and healthy Creative Flow variants — cognitive optimisation shows peak efficacy.
• TRANSFORMING carries the widest spread (std=1.83) with lower mean (6.47), consistent with its ascending-arc targeting low-affect states — higher variance is expected here.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols show analytical tension that warrants investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (n=9) displays notably low mean (5.33) and pct_pos (44%) despite engagement signals — possible efficacy mismatch for its target low-affect state.
• T-CF-L3 shows unusually high pct_pos (67%) at developing n=18 with strong qualitative feedback — early signal suggests strong efficacy that may hold at scale.
• Crisis protocols (H-CR-L1/L2/L3) show 51-53% pct_pos versus 60%+ for other HEALING types — this is structurally expected but may indicate room for tuning.
• T-FC-L1 distribution shows pronounced bimodality — this subgroup effect suggests different listener populations may benefit from different routing.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Multiple analyzers flag X-MC-L3 (n=9, mean=5.33) as requiring redesign — create an L2 variant with richer entrainment or adjust protocol depth for mind clarity applications.
• H-CR-L1 through H-CR-L3 show 51-53% pct_pos, below other HEALING protocols — consider adding additional grounding prompts or adjusting crisis tuning to enhance acute distress support.
• T-FC-L3 shows the highest mean in THRIVING (7.56) with strong positive response — investigate what design elements differentiate it from T-FC-L1 (6.46) and replicate across the Focus series.
• H-OS-L1 has highest n (96) and strong mean (7.10) — retain current design while monitoring YouTube retention data as it emerges.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals significant analytical evolution across runs.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: All 30 protocols show drift below 0.85 similarity, with T-CF-L1 (0.642), H-SL-L3 (0.654), and H-SL-L1 (0.661) showing the highest drift — this indicates that as more data accumulates, Sphere's framing of these protocols is shifting substantially.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Notable pairs include X-MD-L1 ↔ T-CF-L1 (0.946), X-MD-L1 ↔ X-MD-L3 (0.934) — these cross-series clusters suggest similar underlying efficacy patterns.
• Confidence: Only 3 run histories exist, making all embedding patterns provisional — treat as early signals only.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: This anxiety protocol is helping reliably with most listeners settling in about 10 minutes and reporting noticeable improvement. The rhythm feels balanced and calming for the majority, making it a solid option despite low YouTube visibility.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: This variant works well with a solid mean score and most users reporting noticeable improvement within about 5 minutes. It's a reliable option that performs consistently despite limited online discovery.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Based on the qualitative feedback, this protocol appears to help consistently with noticeable improvement reported by most listeners. Its quick settle times and calming rhythm suggest a well-optimized experience.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: This crisis protocol is showing promising signals, with some users reporting significant improvement. The distribution suggests it may work better for certain people in acute distress — which is expected for crisis interventions.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Though labeled for crisis use, this protocol receives mixed music reviews from some users, and its lower mean suggests it may not hit the mark for everyone in acute states. Future optimization could help.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: The immediate settling reported by some users is unusual and promising for a crisis protocol. Its lower mean may reflect the challenging state people are in when they start listening.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: With nearly 100 responses, this overstimulation protocol shows strong consistent results. Most listeners like the music and report noticeable improvement — it's a reliable option that's worth more visibility.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: This variant works well based on qualitative feedback, with most users liking the music and reporting improvement. Its slightly longer settle times may mean it takes a bit longer to calm the system.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: This protocol's higher mean score and very positive music opinions suggest it's resonating well, especially with listeners trying to sleep. It shows reliable calming effects.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: This sleep protocol gets reliable positive feedback, with most users reporting noticeable to moderate improvement. Quick settle times suggest effective initial relaxation — encouraging for sleep applications.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: While this protocol has good scores, some rhythm feedback suggests it might be too busy for optimal sleep. Future versions could explore softer rhythms to see if efficacy improves.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: This sleep protocol stands out with high positive feedback despite smaller sample size. Most users report noticeable improvement and calming rhythm experiences.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: This creative flow protocol shows modest early results with moderate improvement for most users. With only 2 YouTube views, it's an undiscovered option that may deserve more promotion.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Though underutilized online, this protocol's higher mean and faster settle times suggest it's an effective creative flow aid. Qualitative feedback on music and rhythm is positive overall.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: This protocol's unusually high positive response rate at low n suggests very strong early efficacy. Its enthusiastic qualitative feedback warrants consideration for wider promotion.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: This focus protocol has a lower mean than some siblings in the series. Its rhythm feedback is more varied, suggesting possible mismatch with some listener preferences.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: With tight distribution and moderate improvement reports, this focus protocol seems reliable though not exceptional. Its longer settle times are noteworthy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: This protocol stands out with very high mean scores and enthusiastic feedback. Many users report significant improvement and love the music — it seems to be a top-performing focus aid.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: This maintenance protocol's longer settle times and modest improvement reports suggest it may be better suited to subtle long-term balancing than quick interventions.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: While this protocol has good scores, some rhythm feedback suggests possible overstimulation. Future versions could test softer entrainment patterns.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: This protocol's mixed settle responses suggest variability in its effectiveness. Many report noticeable improvement, but some don't settle — worth following up to understand why.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: This mind clarity protocol shows reliable results with noticeable improvement reports. Its faster settle times are encouraging for a transforming series entry.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: This variant is working well with many users reporting significant improvement. Qualitative feedback indicates very settling rhythm experiences — promising for clarity work.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: This protocol's low mean and positive response suggest possible efficacy issues. Despite good engagement signals, it may not be hitting the mark for its target low-affect state.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: This motivation protocol's wide distribution suggests possible subgroup effects. While many report improvement, settle times vary widely, indicating it may work better in specific contexts.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: This variant is showing promising early results with faster settle times than expected for an ascent protocol. It might be particularly effective for study or relaxation contexts.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: This protocol stands out with a high mean despite small n, though the wide distribution suggests potential variability. Its improvement reports warrant scaling with careful monitoring.
